question_html:
Re-write the sentences with the right form of verbs:

(a) Neither Talha nor his brothers (be) present in the family meeting yesterday.

(b) I was looking forward to (hear) from you.

(c) The poor (be) always neglected by society.

(d) Ammar despises (take) exercise.

(e) She got me (fix) her computer.
solution_html:
(a) Neither Talha nor his brothers were present in the family meeting yesterday.

(b) I was looking forward to hearing from you.

(c) The poor are always neglected by society.

(d) Ammar despises taking exercise.

(e) She got me to fix her computer.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for writing the sentences with the correct right form of verbs.

question_html:
Summarize the poem below:

I will arise and go now, and go to Innisfree,

And a small cabin build there, of clay and wattles made:

Nine bean-rows will I have there, a hive for the honeybee,

And live alone in the bee-loud glade.

And I shall have some peace there, for peace comes dropping slow,

Dropping from the veils of the morning to where the cricket sings;

There midnight’s all a glimmer, and noon a purple glow,

And evening full of the linnet’s wings.

I will arise and go now, for always night and day

I hear lake water lapping with low sounds by the shore;

While I stand on the roadway, or on the pavements grey,

I hear it in the deep heart’s core.
solution_html:
Summary:

The speaker longs to escape busy life and live in peace at Innisfree. He imagines a quiet cabin, natural beauty, and the gentle rhythm of bees, birds, and lake water. Though in the city, he carries the sound of Innisfree within his heart. The poem shows the deep human desire for solitude and peace through nature.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for mentioning desire to escape.

01 mark for referring to nature and peaceful life.

01 mark for showing emotional connection to Innisfree.

Instructions:

** Students have to write within 55 words. If write more than that, 0.5 mark will be deducted.

Comment: Write not more than one third of the text/ Write within one third of the text.

** For one or two (spelling or grammatical) mistakes 0.5 mark has to be deducted and three or more (spelling or grammatical) mistakes 01 mark has to be deducted.

question_html: Write down a short paragraph on ‘Price Hike’.
solution_html:
‘Price Hike’

Price hike, or the increase in the cost of daily essential commodities, is one of the most talked-about issues at present. In our country, it has become a regular phenomenon. The prices of daily necessities are soaring sky-high and going beyond the reach of the poor and lower-middle-class people. There are several causes behind this price hike. Hoarding of essential goods by dishonest businessmen is one of the major reasons. Smuggling and black marketing by traders and others are also significant contributors to the price rise. Many dishonest individuals have earned huge amounts of black money through these illegal means, causing great suffering to the public. Shortage of supply due to low production further accelerates the price hike of essential items. At present, syndicate-based business operations have worsened the situation. Due to inflation, the crisis is more acute in our country. The government's mechanism to curb price hike is often ineffective. Many poor villagers cannot afford two square meals a day. As a result, they frequently suffer from malnutrition and other diseases, and some even die without receiving proper medical treatment. Public awareness against illegal hoarders, smugglers, and black marketeers is essential to remove this curse from the country.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for writing the title: Price Hike

01 mark for explaining what price hike is and its current relevance.

1.5 marks for describing the causes such as hoarding, smuggling, black marketing, and syndicates.

01 mark for explaining the effects on poor people and their health.

01 mark for suggesting solutions like public awareness and government action.

Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks must be deducted from the deserved marks.

* Students must write it within the 100 word-limit. For exceeding the 100 words limit, 0.5 marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two (spelling or grammatical) mistakes and 01 mark for three or more (spelling or grammatical) mistakes.
